name: Change Management
description: Production changes are documented, approved, tested, and deployed under the change management process before being released. This control ensures that unauthorised or untested changes cannot reach production and creates an auditable record of who approved what, when, and against which policy.

attributes.text: Every production change has a corresponding change request that documents scope, risk classification, and rollback plan
testable_criteria: A change request exists for the production change
The change request has a change request identifier following the CHG-YYYY-NNNN format
The change request documents scope, including systems affected, code repositories, and config surfaces
The change request documents a risk classification of Low, Medium, or High
The change request documents a rollback plan
The change request identifies a named requester
The change request identifies a named approver
The change request documents planned start and end times
The change is categorised as Standard, Normal, or Emergency

attributes.text: Production changes are approved by an authorised change approver prior to deployment
testable_criteria: The change request has a recorded approval
The approver is a different named human from the requester (four-eyes principle)
The approver holds the Change Approver role in the identity provider
IF the change is Standard THEN at least 1 approver (Change Coordinator) approved the change
IF the change is Normal THEN at least 2 approvers approved the change (Change Coordinator + Change Approver from CAB), and CAB review is evidenced
IF the change is Emergency THEN a Duty Change Approver approved the change AND a retrospective CAB review occurred within 5 business days
For Standard and Normal changes, approval timestamp is before the deployment window opens
IF the approval is retrospective THEN the change is documented as an Emergency change

attributes.text: Pre-deployment testing is completed and evidence retained before release
testable_criteria: IF the change is Standard or Normal THEN a test plan is attached to the change request
IF the change is Standard or Normal THEN test results are attached to the change request
IF the change is Standard or Normal THEN testing evidence pre-dates the deployment
Testing evidence includes, at minimum, functional smoke tests of the affected surfaces
IF any tests failed for a Standard or Normal change THEN deployment did not proceed
IF the change is Emergency THEN post-deployment verification test evidence was captured within 24 hours of deployment
IF any tests failed for an Emergency change THEN the failures are documented as accepted risk
